tags.note: Western tip of Isla Canales de Afuera. Coords derived from island position (7.6922, -81.6231) per getamap.net; Faro/lighthouse is at western exposed tip. Strong currents, bull sharks, big eye jacks.

UNCERTAIN: Not a well-documented Coiba dive site. Original note said Machete Caves is actually in Bocas del Toro. Coords placed near Isla Coibita area pending verification. May need replacement with a verified Coiba site.
Islet NE of Coiba Island. Coords verified: Google Maps 7.5933/-81.7124, snorkeling-report.com 7.593368/-81.713267. Shallow reef around tiny island, excellent snorkeling and diving.
Near Isla Coibita/Rancheria (7.645, -81.703 per mapcarta.com). Cathedral-like rock formations with walls, overhangs and channels. 12-30m depth. Coords derived from Rancheria island position.
Bay on east side of Isla Coiba. Coords verified: getamap.net Lat 7.45, Lon -81.6833. Second largest coral reef in Eastern Pacific (135 hectares).
Isla Canal de Afuera coords verified: getamap.net Lat 7.69222, Lon -81.6231. Most-visited dive area in park. Strong currents, pelagic sharks, whale sharks in season.
Coords verified from multiple sources: getamap.net 7.21333/-81.8025, geoyp.com 7.21158/-81.79952. Southernmost island in Coiba National Park, off tip of Jicaron.
Shallow coral dive near NE Coiba coast. Coords estimated from described location between Granito de Oro and Coibita. Described as gentle shallow hard coral gardens.
Coords verified: PADI dive site database 7.906614/-81.816730. Deep pinnacle in Contreras area, 14-42m, severe currents. Hammerheads, bull sharks, giant mantas.
UNCERTAIN: Not a commonly documented Coiba dive site name. Coords estimated near central-west Coiba coast. May correspond to an operator-specific site name. Needs field verification.
